entries.name: Prime Notes Structuring Assistant

It extracts only stated information, removes fluff, marks missing details as "Not Discussed," and outputs clean, consistent documentation using a fixed internal template.

entries.name: Data Quote Assistant

entries.name: Friday Case Comment Assistant
entries.manual: Manually reviewing Salesforce notes/internal updates and drafting weekly Friday case comments. Removes rep effort spent condensing status, engagement recency, next steps, and missing details into a clean Salesforce-ready update.
entries.outcome: Faster, more consistent case summaries with clearer customer engagement visibility, cleaner handoffs, and reduced rep editing time. Improves weekly case hygiene and helps identify stalled/unresponsive cases sooner.
entries.artifact: Salesforce case comment / weekly case summary
entries.goal: Reduce admin time; improve case hygiene and consistency; increase CS/support efficiency; surface at-risk/unresponsive cases faster

entries.name: Onboarding Case Closure Note
entries.manual: Describe the human task this GPT replaces or reduces. For this GPT, that would likely be something like: reviewing onboarding case comments and PRIME notes, then drafting a concise Salesforce-ready case closure summary.
entries.outcome: State the business result created by the GPT. For example: improves consistency of onboarding closure notes, reduces rep time spent writing summaries, and helps ensure cleaner CS handoffs.
entries.artifact: What the GPT produces. In this case, likely: Salesforce case closure note, case comment draft, or closure summary.
entries.goal: The higher-level business objective. For this GPT, likely examples are: reduce onboarding admin time, improve handoff quality, increase consistency, reduce avoidable churn risk, or improve NPS/retention through better transitions.

entries.name: Automate Training call notes and emails
entries.manual: After every onboarding or training call, reps manually write Salesforce case notes, post them as case comments, compose customer recap emails, and look up service package progress — all done by hand after each session. This automation replaces all of it.
entries.outcome: Saves reps 20–30 minutes per onboarding call. Ensures consistent case note and email quality across the entire team, reduces human error in Salesforce documentation, and delivers professional customer recap emails faster. Gives leadership visibility into every rep's daily onboarding activity via automated Slack reporting.
entries.artifact: Salesforce Case Comment, Gmail Draft, Slack Run Report
entries.goal: Scale without headcount, Reduce TTV, Standardize onboarding quality

entries.name: Daily Collections Brief
entries.tools: Claude (Cowork mode), Intercom, Gmail, Google Drive
entries.manual: Each morning, manually opening Intercom, scanning Elenna's open queue, identifying expired extensions, unassigned tickets, paid-but-open anomalies, $1K+ MMR watch items, customer-waiting tickets, comparing against yesterday's open list to spot anything untouched, then writing a structured email to Elenna with action items + snapshot. ~2 hours per weekday.
entries.outcome: Faster collections follow-up, fewer tickets aging silently, named accountability on stale items, daily visibility into MMR at risk without me having to dig.
entries.artifact: Gmail draft (review-then-send) along with live dashboard with clickable links in Claude
entries.goal: Scale without headcount + reduce DSO / improve cash recovery

entries.name: Weekly Collections MRR Report
entries.manual: Opening the Monday Intercom scheduled-report email, clicking through to the snapshot, exporting the CSV, filtering to the prior Sunday–Saturday window in Mountain Time, calculating the three metrics, formatting the standard email to John. ~45 min per run.
entries.outcome: Consistent, on-time weekly leadership reporting on Collections MMR — no missed Mondays, no math errors, no "I'll get to it after standup."
entries.artifact: Gmail draft (review-then-send)
entries.goal: Scale without headcount + leadership visibility into Collections performance

entries.name: MRR Dashboard
entries.manual: Combining two 10K-row IQ transaction exports, deleting 22+ unused columns, and matching invoices to product data by hand each month. Manually calculating first attempt date, approval date, days-to-approve, and bucket assignments per invoice. Hunting fail reasons across auth response codes and grouping them for reporting. Rebuilding the dashboard and writing the MBR synopsis from scratch every month. Saves roughly 20 hours a week
entries.outcome: Produces a defensible, repeatable monthly collections analysis with consistent metric definitions for MoM and QoQ trending. Cuts MBR prep from days to hours and surfaces fail-reason patterns early enough to act on them.
entries.artifact: Google Sheets workbook + dashboard + MBR synopsis
entries.goal: Scales without headcount; reduces time-to-insight for MBR reporting.

entries.name: MBR Deck
entries.manual: Previously, building the monthly Collections MBR required ~4-6 hours of manual labor: pulling data from four disparate systems (Fullbay workbook, Stripe, Altus portal, MRR tracker), cross-referencing prior-month numbers in PDF decks, hand-building charts in Canva, writing speaker notes, and reconciling discrepancies when late retries posted. This project consolidates all of that into a single workflow that ingests the workbook + screenshots, auto-reconciles against the prior deck, calculates working estimates for missing inputs, and produces a finished, brand-aligned, presentation-ready .pptx with native editable charts. The Director and CCO get the same polished deliverable every month without the analyst burning a half-day formatting bars and hunting for hex codes. This easily saves 4 - 6 hours.
entries.outcome: Cuts Collections MBR prep from ~5 hours to under 30 minutes — an ~85-90% time reduction — while increasing consistency, brand alignment, and data-integrity flagging (reconciliation warnings, placeholder tracking, anomaly callouts). Leadership gets a faster, more reliable read on revenue exposure, recovery speed, and third-party performance, which means earlier escalation on trend reversals and tighter feedback loops with the Collections team. The analyst (me) gets to spend the reclaimed time on actual analysis instead of fighting Canva alignment guides.
entries.artifact: Native PowerPoint deck (.pptx) with editable charts + supporting CSV chart data + plain-text copy block + end-of-run QA review.
entries.goal: Scale without headcount, Reduce TTV, Improve cross-functional visibility

entries.name: Fin (Spike) Messenger / Chat Contact Channel
entries.freq: Always-on; triggers on every customer or internal user chat initiated in the messenger
entries.manual: First-response handling by support agents for common how-to questions, navigation help, and feature-specific guidance that is already documented in the KB
entries.outcome: Reduced inbound ticket volume to human agents, faster time-to-resolution for end users, and support coverage extended beyond business hours without additional headcount
entries.artifact: AI-generated conversational responses grounded in published KB articles
entries.goal: Scale support capacity without scaling headcount; improve customer self-service and reduce cost-per-contact

entries.name: Inbound Phone Support – Intercom Voice + Fin AI Voice Agent
entries.tools: Intercom (Voice/Phone Channel), Intercom Fin AI Voice
entries.freq: Fin Voice is always-on during nights and weekends. During business hours, Fin Voice triggers automatically on any inbound call that exceeds the team's pickup rate SLA — ensuring no call goes unanswered regardless of queue depth or agent availability
entries.manual: After-hours on-call coverage, manual callback scheduling for abandoned or queued calls, and first-contact handling for overflow volume during peak business hours. Agents are no longer the first line of response for every inbound call — Fin absorbs the volume that exceeds capacity.
entries.outcome: Elimination of missed calls during off-hours and reduction in call abandonment during business hours. Customers receive an immediate response at any hour rather than hitting a voicemail or being left in queue. As Fin Voice expands to cover SLA-breach calls during business hours, it is expected to deflect a meaningful portion of routine inbound volume — freeing Tier 1 agents to focus on higher-complexity calls that require human judgment. This also reduces the pressure on staffing models during peak periods, as Fin provides elastic capacity that scales with call volume without additional headcount. Long term, consistent SLA adherence on phone becomes achievable without growing the team proportionally to demand.
entries.artifact: AI-handled voice conversation + Intercom conversation record
entries.goal: Eliminate call abandonment, maintain phone SLA without headcount scaling, and extend consistent support coverage to 24/7

entries.name: Email Support – Fin AI Agent on Intercom Email Channel
entries.tools: Email Support – Fin AI Agent on Intercom Email Channel
entries.manual: First-touch email triage, initial response drafting for answerable questions, and manual routing decisions. For the Fin resolves outright, the entire human handling cycle is eliminated. For escalated emails, agents inherit a structured conversation rather than a raw email, reducing time spent on intake and categorization.
entries.outcome: Fin serves as the first responder on every inbound email, ensuring customers receive an immediate touchpoint regardless of agent availability or queue depth. Emails that Fin resolves never reach the human queue, returning capacity back to the team on a channel that historically required full agent handling for every single contact. For emails that do escalate, agents inherit a structured, contextualized conversation rather than a raw email thread, reducing the time spent on intake before meaningful work can begin.
entries.goal: Reduce agent email handling volume, improve first-response time on the email channel, and grow self-service resolution rate

entries.name: Professional Service Scoping Assistant
entries.manual: Scoping and implementation Statement of Work, pricing, and quote assistant for all customers and internal employees
entries.outcome: Reduce confusion on what is in scope with standard implementation packages as well as what is available to upsell to customers
entries.artifact: App
